text: Meeting deadlines is a critical workplace skill that requires effective time management. Start by breaking large projects into smaller tasks and setting mini-deadlines for each. Prioritization is equally important—identify which tasks are urgent versus important, and focus on those first. Remember scope management: if extra requests arise, politely clarify if they change the original deadline. Finally, communication is key. If a deadline becomes impossible, inform your team or client early, offering a revised timeline. This honesty builds trust and allows for adjustments before problems escalate. With these strategies, you can handle deadlines calmly and professionally.
